rows.requirement: Get Order MUST return the full order entity as a current-state snapshot (complete record of what was purchased, how it will be delivered, and what has happened since placement), reflecting line items, fulfillment, and event logs
rows.quote:
It provides a complete record of what was purchased, how
it will be delivered, and what has happened since order placement.
rows.notes: Get Order returns the authoritative full entity snapshot. Oracle: .vendor/conformance/order_test.py OrderTest.test_order_retrieval asserts GET /orders/{id} returns 200 with matching order id and checkout_id. Schema validates entity shape but cannot enforce full-vs-partial. 'MUST' implied by the spec's normative requirement that updates send the full order entity (L339); snapshot completeness is data-state, not schema-expressible.

rows.requirement: The order entity MUST include the required top-level fields ucp, id, checkout_id, permalink_url, line_items, fulfillment, and totals
rows.notes: Top-level order.json required[] in the 2026-01-23 tree does NOT include 'currency' (currency became required only in the 2026-04-08 order.json). 'adjustments' is optional in both. JSON-Schema enforced.

rows.requirement: currency MUST NOT be treated as a required top-level order field in spec version 2026-01-23 (currency is absent from order.json required[])
rows.notes: Version-skew guard: in 2026-04-08 order.json 'currency' was added to required[]; in 2026-01-23 it is NOT present, so an order without top-level currency is schema-valid here. Same verbatim required[] block as ORD-002.

rows.requirement: Each order line item MUST include id, item, quantity, totals, and status
rows.notes: order_line_item.json required[]. quantity object itself requires total+fulfilled (ORD-005). status enum = processing|partial|fulfilled. JSON-Schema enforced.

rows.requirement: Line item quantity MUST include both total and fulfilled counts
rows.notes: quantity sub-object required[]; both integers minimum 0. total/fulfilled are derived from fulfillment events; status is derived (fulfilled==total -> fulfilled, fulfilled>0 -> partial, else processing).

rows.requirement: Line items reflect what was purchased at checkout and MUST be retained as the immutable source of truth for what was ordered
rows.quote: Immutable line items — source of truth for what was ordered.
rows.notes: Line-item retention/immutability is a data-state property described in order.json line_items.description and docs L51 ('reflect what was purchased at checkout and their current state'); not JSON-Schema enforceable. Order entity description (order.json L7) also says 'immutable line items'.

rows.requirement: Adjustments are an append-only log; each adjustment MUST include id, type, occurred_at, and status
rows.notes: adjustment.json required[]. type is an open string; status enum = pending|completed|failed; line_items and amount optional. Oracle: .vendor/conformance/order_test.py OrderTest.test_order_adjustments asserts a refund adjustment is retained with amount==500 and type=='refund'.

rows.requirement: Adjustments are an append-only log of events that exist independently of fulfillment and can happen at any time regardless of fulfillment status
rows.quote:
**Adjustments** are an append-only log of events that exist independently of
fulfillment:
rows.notes: Append-only / independent-of-fulfillment semantics (docs L87-96: 'Can happen at any time regardless of fulfillment status'). Append-only ledger ordering is data-state, not schema-expressible. Same adjustments array exercised by OrderTest.test_order_adjustments.

rows.requirement: Each fulfillment event (append-only log of actual shipments) MUST include id, occurred_at, type, and line_items
rows.notes: fulfillment_event.json required[]; type is an open string; tracking_number/tracking_url are described as required if type != processing (description-only, not schema-enforced). Oracle: order_test.py OrderTest.test_order_update appends a fulfillment event and asserts events[0].tracking_number is retained.

rows.requirement: Each fulfillment expectation (buyer-facing grouping) MUST include id, line_items, method_type, and destination
rows.notes: expectation.json required[]; method_type enum = shipping|pickup|digital. Oracle: order_test.py OrderTest.test_order_fulfillment_retrieval asserts order.fulfillment.expectations is populated and expectation description is retained.

rows.requirement: The platform MUST provide its webhook URL (webhook_url) in the order capability's config field during negotiation; the platform order capability config requires webhook_url
rows.notes: order.json $defs.platform_schema requires webhook_url (string, format uri). docs L273-275: platform provides webhook_url in the order capability config during negotiation; business discovers it from the platform profile. Requires a controllable platform endpoint to observe POSTs (needs-receiver).

rows.requirement: Businesses POST order lifecycle events to the platform-provided webhook URL, sending the full order entity (not incremental deltas)
rows.quote: **MUST** send full order entity on updates (not incremental deltas)
rows.notes: Business Guidelines (docs L333-341). Oracle: .vendor/conformance/webhook_test.py WebhookTest.test_webhook_event_stream asserts order_placed and order_shipped webhook payloads each carry payload.order.id == order_id (full order entity). Receiver-dependent.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST send an 'Order created' event with a fully populated order entity
rows.quote: **MUST** send "Order created" event with fully populated order entity
rows.notes: docs L338. Oracle: webhook_test.py WebhookTest.test_webhook_event_stream asserts an order_placed event is present and its payload.order.id matches the created order (placed maps to 'Order created'). Receiver-dependent.

rows.requirement: Webhook payloads MUST be signed by the business and verified by the platform to ensure authenticity and integrity
rows.quote:
Webhook payloads **MUST** be signed by the business and verified by the platform
to ensure authenticity and integrity.
rows.notes: docs L296-297. Signing/verification is observable only at a controllable receiver. webhook_test.py exercises the event stream but does not assert signature verification, so official_oracle=false.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST sign all webhook payloads using a key from their signing_keys array (published in /.well-known/ucp), and the signature MUST be included in the Request-Signature header as a detached JWT (RFC 7797)
rows.quote:
**MUST** sign all webhook payloads using a key from their `signing_keys`
  array (published in `/.well-known/ucp`). The signature **MUST** be included
  in the `Request-Signature` header as a detached JWT (RFC 7797).
rows.notes: docs L335-337. NOTE the version difference: 2026-01-23 specifies signing via a detached JWT (RFC 7797) in the 'Request-Signature' header and key selection by 'kid' from signing_keys (docs L300-314) — it does NOT use Standard-Webhooks headers (Webhook-Id/Webhook-Timestamp/Webhook-Signature) nor RFC 9421; no such headers appear anywhere in the 2026-01-23 order.md or schemas. Receiver-dependent.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST retry failed webhook deliveries
rows.notes: docs L340. Retry behavior is observable only by inducing failures at a controllable receiver. Not asserted by webhook_test.py.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST include a business identifier in the webhook path or headers
rows.quote: **MUST** include business identifier in webhook path or headers
rows.notes: docs L341. The webhook receiver in webhook_test.py uses a partner-scoped path (/webhooks/partners/test_partner/events/order) but the test does not assert the business-identifier requirement, so official_oracle=false. Receiver-dependent.

rows.requirement: The platform MUST respond quickly with a 2xx HTTP status code to acknowledge receipt of a webhook, then process events asynchronously after responding
rows.quote: **MUST** respond quickly with a 2xx HTTP status code to acknowledge receipt
rows.notes: Platform Guidelines (docs L328-331). 'Process events asynchronously after responding' (L331). The mock webhook server in webhook_test.py returns 2xx, but the test asserts event receipt/content, not the platform's 2xx/async obligation per se, so official_oracle=false. Receiver-dependent. Reclassified 2026-07-03 (webhook/events area): the MUST binds the PLATFORM (order.md Guidelines 'Platform:' block) — the webhook RECEIVER's acknowledgement behavior, not the business under test. Candidate for a documented client/platform-side exemption in Phase 4.

rows.requirement: On webhook verification: the platform extracts Request-Signature, parses the JWT header for kid, fetches the business UCP profile, locates the matching key in signing_keys, and verifies the signature against the request body; if verification fails, it MUST reject the webhook with an appropriate error response
rows.quote: If verification fails, reject the webhook with an appropriate error response.
rows.notes: Verification (Platform) steps, docs L307-314. The signer key is selected by 'kid' from the business's signing_keys published at /.well-known/ucp; rejecting on signature mismatch is the anti-spoof control. Receiver-dependent; not asserted by webhook_test.py. Reclassified 2026-07-03 (webhook/events area): the quoted step is the PLATFORM's verification procedure (order.md 'Verification (Platform)' step 6: the receiver rejects a bad webhook) — receiver-side conduct, not a business obligation observable on the merchant under test. Candidate for a documented client/platform-side exemption in Phase 4.

notes: Spec commit dcf7eac71fc370dcc8768fcdbc5aa737037cca05, version 2026-01-23. Sources: docs/specification/order.md and source/schemas/shopping/order.json + types/{order_line_item,adjustment,fulfillment_event,expectation}.json. Note: the order capability files internally carry version label '2026-01-11' (docs L20, order.json L5) although they live in the 2026-01-23 spec tree.
Get Order returns a full current-state snapshot / full order entity, not deltas (ORD-001); the same full-entity contract is reused on webhook delivery (ORD-012/013).

Order top-level required[] in the 2026-01-23 order.json = ucp, id, checkout_id, permalink_url, line_items, fulfillment, totals (verified verbatim, ORD-002). 'currency' is NOT required here — it was only added to required[] in the 2026-04-08 order.json (ORD-003 captures this version delta). 'adjustments' is optional.
Line-item retention/immutability (ORD-006) and the adjustment append-only ledger (ORD-008) are data-state requirements not expressible in JSON Schema; schema_enforced=false on those.

Webhook rows (ORD-011..019) marked testability=needs-receiver: they require a controllable platform receiver to observe POSTs, signatures, retries, and 2xx acknowledgement. IMPORTANT version delta: 2026-01-23 signs webhooks with a detached JWT (RFC 7797) in the 'Request-Signature' header, key-selected by 'kid' from signing_keys at /.well-known/ucp — it does NOT use Standard-Webhooks headers (Webhook-Id/Webhook-Timestamp) or RFC 9421; those appear in later spec versions, not here (grep confirms zero occurrences in the 2026-01-23 tree).

official_oracle=true where the vendored suite asserts the condition: ORD-001 (order_test.py OrderTest.test_order_retrieval), ORD-007/ORD-008 (OrderTest.test_order_adjustments asserts refund type+amount), ORD-009 (OrderTest.test_order_update asserts retained fulfillment event tracking_number), ORD-010 (OrderTest.test_order_fulfillment_retrieval asserts retained expectation), ORD-012/ORD-013 (webhook_test.py WebhookTest.test_webhook_event_stream asserts placed/shipped events carry the full order entity). The vendored conformance suite at .vendor/conformance targets spec-2026-01-23, so oracles are version-aligned for this register.
